### ICT Relevance:
In ICT methodology, this **10:00 AM EST release** falls within the **Late AM Kill Zone / New York Kill Zone** window. ICT traders specifically **avoid holding positions or entering trades** during high-impact news events like UoM data, as they can cause **liquidity sweeps, stop hunts, and manipulated price delivery** that falls outside clean institutional order flow.

The **downward arrows** on both readings suggest the actual data came in **below expectations**, which could trigger **USD weakness** and inform directional bias for the remainder of the session.

Sorry to cut the stream short guys. Wanted to go over the trade with you so here's the post trade breakdown.

On the HTF, NQ tapped a monthly SSL level yesterday. On the 4h we created an ifvg , hit the daily VI and retraced back into the 4h ifvg.

On the LTF, we were below midnight and 8:30, which is suitable if an A+ long is in play. You want to be below midnight and 8:30am since those time levels can act as magnets but also means you are likely in a discount.

I monitored ES and saw that it had equal lows. So my thesis was that we'd open and potentially PO3 below those lows to set up for a long (since my HTF bias was leaning long after bouncing off 4h ifvg and tapping monthly SSL).

I set an alert on ES at that level and once it swept, I went back to NQ and looked for an aggressive close above the last fvg before the SSL sweep. My target was the last swing high at 18,174.

Also very happy to see y'all taking these trades in MNQ, recognizing that we are in such a high volatility environment, there's no need to risk large losses in NQ, especially if you're in prop. 

We hit over 100 points in a few minutes. 5 MNQ was still $1,000 trade.

Congrats guys 🦾 . Hopefully this helps. @everyone

Huge move up in the overnight session to tap the D VI in NQ before 500 pts down. Already 200 pt swing into the open. Volatility is likely to remain this week. Trading volatility in itself can be fine as long you adjust your risk parameters - ie, move your stop and TPs double or triple what's normal, size WAY down so your dollar risk remains constant, but volatility can skew the probability of what we expect to occur in PA - meaning wicks can violate PDAs we expect to normally be respected. Be very careful this week, micros, sim, observing, or staying out completely. My bias for now is potentially a move to the GP, but really anything can happen here. Trying to predict the open is going to be a losing game. Going to be observe, but as volatility calms, be more aggressive with my positioning. **[06/03/2024 1:04 PM]**
I want to reflect on this trade as every losing trade should be reviewed and dissected. This was the correct bias but entry was the problem. We had three potential high-confidence levels of bouncing from: the flipped D VI, the breaker (🦄), or the GP of the range. We have to balance how optimal our entry is with the probability of getting filled. I opted for the middle ground of all three entries as I believed in the setup and wanted to ensure participation in a move up. What could have tipped me off is the quicker we would have seen the reversal, the more bullish we would have been. Because I didn't get filled until lunch, potentially opting for the most conservative entry would have been wise. We always need to tweak our biases and models, cause even with the right idea, bottom ticks can happen. Onto the next one @everyone

$0 to pass in 1 day - 50ks 

Going to illustrate my new scalping strat today in real time by attempting to take 13 fresh 50k Apex evals from $0 to pass in one day. I will use 8 cons, aim for 3-8 pts per scalp, with suboptimal R/R in order to let the unrealized intraday drawdown work in my favor. This method looks for quick, high probability, safe trades in consolidation where you won't get caught on the opposite side of expansion. Below is an example of the A+ conditions for this strat. You want barcode consolidation, and then look to enter near the edges of the range - either equal levels or 1m RBs, while paying attention to market structure to anticipate a break from the range. 

I use the 1m to develop my bias, while referencing 5/15m PDAs. I then use NT 15s with pre-loaded one-click ATMs to limit in. I NEVER market on this strategy as often the slippage you'll receive with market is the entire move you're looking to capture.

There are also times I look to grab the countertrend move to exhaustive expansion, but that is far riskier, as you have to be familiar with where price is likely to stop expanding (I've noticed it's usually a combo of standard deviations + eq levels). 

The trades are very fast, but I'm going to call out the ranges I am interested in playing, and post my P&L as I take each trade. Will also post the entries if there is enough advance preparation, but the goal is for these trades to fill entry and exit in less than 1 minute. Feel free to follow along, and hoping the day doesn't end in me just burning 13 evals publicly 

@everyone

Very rare occurrence that the S&P is in 3 major fvgs: weekly, daily, and hourly. Unemployment came in slightly lower than expected at 3.7% vs 3.8% and payrolls came in much higher at +216K vs +175K estimate.

The market rarely sees 5 red days in a row. In the last 3.5 years it happened 4 times. Every single one of those times saw a violent rally thereafter. Not to mention that Fridays can see a TGIF setup, which I will likely be looking for today. @everyone 

On the 5m, you can see we dipped below Asia low, formed a potential PO3, taking out London SSL at the 8:30am open. Not taking a trade here yet but this is looking interesting for a potential TGIF setup, especially since all this happened in major FVGs. It is NFP day though, so tread lightly.

We took key draws in the overnight session - NQ taking July highs/monthly buyside, and ES mitigating its daily FVG and D VI. We're now on our 7th green weekly candle, which is incredibly abnormal. Good morning traders! Today is FOMC day. The Fed will give us its decision at 2pm EST with Powell taking the podium at 2:30pm EST.

As expected, the S&P and Nasdaq are digging into that daily FVG, created by two of the worst 1-day drops. ES mitigated 50% of it, NQ is a bit further away. If today wasn't FOMC day I'd be bullish early with a tilt towards bearishness after both indices mitigate that FVG. In normal circumstances I would also be looking for a multi day short on SPY and QQQ.

However, October is over and November/December are usually the two best months in the stock market. Shorting every pop will work until it doesn't and unless AAPL earnings shit the bed tomorrow, it's a tough place to open a heavy short. I don't mind hedging if you have a heavy portfolio but at this point tough to open a large short position.

Price action in the premarket was surprisingly clean, with both indice taking out premarket lows before a move up. Right now I'm not looking to trade the indices until FOMC starts, since the bulk of the move has been made premarket. A reminder guys, this week is obviously super news heavy. We have HTF objectives directly above and below us keeping us range bound. I believe this week we will stay in this range building up liquidity over time until FOMC (tomorrow) and unemployment (friday) where both sides will be swept. Trades this week should be with small risk and only A+. 

NFP Week is usually some of this most erratic PA causing trades to be limited and of higher risk. This was the A+ today I saw and it even was full of manipulation. Longing "V Shaped" PA is really hard to do with confidence as it could easily impulse through your entry and Stop Loss. This was a great example of po3 into entry.

The biggest benefit you can do for yourself this week is to not focus on big wins but rather focus on ending everyday green this week. If you can do that, even ending $50 green per day, you can consider your week crushed.
@everyone

Good morning traders! Yesterday was a suboptimal day, as most Mondays are during non-farm payrolls week (data released on Friday). We also have FOMC tomorrow, and AAPL earnings on Thursday. 

From a daily bias perspective, we are still very much in a bearish MS on the HTF. However, that does not mean you won't have green days within the bearish MS. In this case we are bouncing off of daily imbalances on the daily chart; ES bounced from a daily FVG and NQ bounced from a daily VI. Yesterday NQ mitigated its VI but there's a huge fvg right above. ES didn't mitigate the VI yet, and also has a huge fvg right above. 

I can see a case for wanting to go higher before FOMC tomorrow in general but the lower timeframe doesn't support that yet. On the lower timeframes, both ES and NQ spiked above midnight and true NY open (7:30am EST). Also shifted MS to bearish. So far, based on the lower timeframe structure and the imbalances on the higher timeframe, I'm leaning slightly bearish for the day.

But, keep in mind, we've seen pre-FOMC days be an utter chop fest, in anticipation of the big day. Either way, if we take positions today I won't be sized heavy. @everyone 

Any questions lemme know 🙂

Wanted to give a recap on how I approached my AM Momentum strat this morning. I am close to a full write up with my backtesting set nearly concluded, but will continue to break it down daily now so people can see daily examples. 

 I had to have entered a broader trade by the close of the 9:55am 5m candle (vertical line). Price opened up just minutes later. I ended up re-submitting my stop limit order (it's a trip entry, essentially a breakout order triggered when price breaks a certain level) despite the official strat time running out as I still thought there was a chance we dump. I wanted to place that limit where a long would have been invalidated and where I would expect continuation in the opposite direction - below opening run OB.

 I entered at 06 short, aiming for us to violently break out of the D barcode we're in. I knew there was a high chance it would be stuck in this range. My trade saw about 50 pts, but I was aiming for massive 200 pt break that sees continuation from our bearishness on the daily. I ended up bailing only 10 pts up by the time it reversed. The 5m invalidated based on the character of its candle close (no wick at the top, displacement up), so an official alert would have only seen 10pts or a b/e when following the strat. Unless of course you took profit on the way down on your own. I'm also experimenting with a 3R first trim and comparing that vs holding full position to larger targets. 

 I'll continue to adjust the time in which the strat can trigger an entry, but this model saves me consolidation, only letting massive runners run and avoiding any broader chop/S&D. Many days will be nothing-burgers for the strat, but when it hits, massive runs are captured It's a very stress-free way to capture large point moves in this market @everyone

- The **outer larger shaded zone (~15,180–15,290)

Monitoring to see what's possible and continuing to improve parameters in the strat. The mitigation of the daily FVG was my runners target - 250pts, daily SSL my primary target - 130 pts. In terms of entry and exit criteria I am monitoring the 5m. The green candle close on the 5 made me exit my position (1st blue box), but as stated in my prior post, I could have likely stayed in due to market consolidating for PMI. I shorted near the lows in that range later as I still believed in the setup, and exited once I got an impulsive 5m close above a first primary target - D OB with ES near the .5 of its D FVG (2nd blue box). Lastly I could have stayed in the position for the primary target by only exiting once I saw a 5m candle close over the primary target - D SSL, leaving only runners for the FVG mitigation if I still believed it's possible (3rd blue box). I find in this strat monitoring the 5 will help me judge momentum and achieve maximum point value. In my backtesting I found myself wanting to exit on primary targets (maybe 50-100 pts), whereas if I went by momentum on the 5 that's what allowed me to capture the 200pt days. Exits upon reversal confirmation mean you're retracing from peak profit, but way more likely to hold for insane runs, which is what makes the strat profitable. Finishing my backtesting and will do a full write up this week. But I've already caught 90 pts on Friday, b/e yesterday, and 70 pts today with a very late entry @everyone

As mentioned in https://discord.com/channels/400345242882146314/946491552425971782/1147232379778367498 I am working on a new AM model that requires me to take a trade daily in the first 15 minutes. I'll post a full write up with my completed data this week, the short version is this strat is designed to catch the giant 200+ pt days we've been seeing on NQ. It requires a move to the 5m to judge momentum, with much broader targets and much broader stops. Many days end in breakevens/small wins/small losses cause momentum didn't produce a large enough move, but there are enough 100+ pt moves captured to pay for -30 pt stops, the occasional 50 pt stop. I'm going to alert this today - my methods for entry are either market or limit depending on range and volatility. Wanted to illustrate precisely why price has been tougher this week. Price has been delivering non-optimally in a way that makes broader moves and even scalps tougher to catch with sound risk management. This morning we only had a very shallow retracement not even to yesterday's lunch lows - despite that, it made the run for weekly buyside (box 1). If offered no key retracements on the way up. Once taken, if you were short biased, you'd look for a confirmation (break of MS, displacement, etc) to then look for a retracement to enter short. It offered no retracement, not even a 1m FVG in a 150 pt dive to sellside (box 2). Impossible to catch unless you simply market in on a red candle, which over time, is a losing strategy. Once we took local sellside, we would want to see displacement up to look for an entry on the 1m. There were zero imbalances created to the upside, making it look as though it's simply a retracement up, and it wants further rungs of sellside below. Not only did it not create any FVGs on LTFs, it didn't even retrace to an OTE, let alone the .5 (box 3). By then it looks as though it likely wants to go retest HOD, but it already made it to the GP then started chopping. It entered seek & destroy (S&D) in its run to highs that took nearly 2 hours and has been inconsistent in retracements - sometimes deep OTE, sometimes a .5 S/R flip, meaning it's very difficult to secure a higher entry without getting stopped with tighter risk management (box 4). This PA is dangerous and why Z, Justin, and myself have been finding it harder to manage risk and capture broader moves in this style of delivery. It is ALWAYS better to preserve your capital and wait for optimal trades, despite it being challenging from a discipline perspective when you spend extended time engaging with price. @everyone (edited)

**[08/25/2023 1:05 PM]**
I ended up not being able to take the trade I waited for all morning for due to NT, but I do think it's helpful to show exactly why I had heavy conviction in that move. My bias was derived from the daily chart. The fact that we printed the most bearish candle in over a year on NQ had me looking for downside only. Starting at the daily, I saw we violently rejected the daily OB, thereby creating the right shoulder in a H&S pattern. From a HTF, I am now looking for pdLow, daily SSL, and weekly SSL to be taken next. Moving into a LTF (15m) I saw clear draws below (RTH gaps, pdLow), with a manipulation move up in the premarket. I was waiting for J Powell volatility with a false move, and once we moved up and swept pd PM BSL, all I needed was a sharp move down. My plan was to market in, as we haven't been seeing any significant retracements. I tried to market around 14970s when I realized NT was delayed. I had my TPs below, with a final runners TP at weekly sellside (the only one not to hit). I made sure to be INCREDIBLY selective with my trades rest of day. Because when you miss your planned A+ trade, the worst thing you can do is risk on sub-par trades after. The PM only presented scalps for me. Hoping this top-down analysis of the bias from this morning was helpful. Many more to catch next week. Have a wonderful weekend traders @everyone

> **[Chart 3]** ## Economic Calendar Analysis – August 23, 2023

This image is **not a trading chart** — it is an **economic calendar** for **Wednesday, August 23, 2023**, displaying USD-denominated macro data releases with scheduled times, actual results, forecasts, and previous readings.

---

### Key Data Points Relevant to ICT Traders:

1. **S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI (Aug)** at 09:45 — Actual **47.0** (red, missed forecast of 49.3), indicating **contraction** and a significant bearish surprise
2. **S&P Global US Services PMI (Aug)** at 09:45 — Actual **51.0** vs forecast **52.3**, also a **bearish miss**
3. **New Home Sales (Jul)** at 10:00 — Actual **714K** (green, beat forecast of 705K), a **bullish beat**
4. **Crude Oil Inventories** at 10:30 — Actual **-6.135M** vs forecast **-2.850M**, a **significant draw** (bullish for crude)

---

### ICT Relevance:
In ICT methodology, this calendar would be used to **identify News Kill Zones** and potential **manipulation/expansion candles**
Some info on NQ/ES this AM:
My bias coming into open was short, after reaching D Bearish FVG's on NQ in an OTE with our overall bearish trend on HTFs I was confident we would see a rejection after some form of BSL grab in a Judas and see a solid shorting opportunity on NQ in the AM session. We didn't get that as you can see (aided by heavy impact news drop this AM) and from the looks of it the current DOL is pHOD on ES in my opinion. My only optimal trade out of this current PA is either continuation scalps until ES takes pHOD or a 5m+ MSS occurs, or a short after a HTF MSS. I took 2 small scalps to cancel each other out so far, 1 trying to catch a top with a light position which i took a small stop on and 1 continuation long on NQ AFTER internal SSL was taken. In my opinion when we miss the boat like this and news sends us higher than expected, the optimal longs are scalps to previous swing highs / significant BSL after a swing low has been taken. Here is an example of the long I took this AM on NQ. I covered my last runners after ES took significant BSL. Have been watching the indices today looking for a broader move. So far price has swung in a larger pennant, but crept with few larger retracements. I find days like today make for excellent scalping. Go WITH trend, always, unless you have 1m confirmation from a reaction off confluence from very strong levels. Trend will also vary across different timeframes. I recommend weighing the D, 4h, 1h, 15m, 5m, and 1m. Generally you want to be scalping with displacement, vs against it. Note the bearish SMT (smart money) divergence between /ES and /NQ. Nasdaq is making new highs with /ES is unable to even approach a larger imbalance on a HTF. This divergence signals possible weakness in the laggard - /ES. I'm going to keep watching for a broader move in the PM session, but will call out any high probability scalps in # unknown-channel. **[10/11/2022 11:19 AM]**
> **[Chart]** ## Chart Analysis

### 1. Ticker, Timeframe & Date/Time
This is the **S&P 500 Index (SP)** on a **5-minute timeframe**, captured on **October 11, 2022 at 11:17 UTC-7**, with price at **3619.70**. The chart spans from October 10 through October 12, covering multiple trading sessions including the **New York Kill Zone**.

### 2. ICT Concepts Visible
- **Order Blocks (OBs):** Multiple gray/teal shaded rectangles are visible on October 10 and 11, marking bearish and bullish order blocks where price reacted — consistent with ICT's mitigation block concept
- **Displacement & BOS:** A sharp bullish displacement move occurs around the 08:00–09:00 window on October 11, representing a **Break of Structure (BOS)** to the upside after sweeping **sell-side liquidity (SSL)** at the prior lows (~3568)
- **ChoCH:** The circled candle (brown/gold circle) near 09:00 marks a likely **Change of Character**, signaling the shift from bearish to bullish delivery
- **Premium/Discount (OTE Levels):** Fibonacci retracement levels are drawn from
So here was my mistake with this trade. I prematurely took it after hitting this swing high, when there was way more liquidity likely waiting higher (the rectangles on the left). Yes, it dropped down violently and retraced back into and FVG, but there was still more liquidity to be taken up top. Now, liquidity doesn't necessarily always get taken out immediately but this makes it a less optimal trade with so much possibility of retracing higher. Plus, it puts me in a bad spot for an exit: with the potential for liquidity grabs right above I'd be forced to wait to see what happens after liquidity is taken, which could kill the option premiums (especially on 0DTEs). 

Another thing is I didn't have confluence for the trade, besides a nice retracement into a lowly 5m FVG. Wasn't at a key Fib level or trendline. 

I also should've waited for market structure to turn bearish on the 15m and up timeframe. MS was still bullish. 

What I should've done:

- Even if I was going to front-run an actual buyside liquidity grab and MS shift, I shouldn't have entered a trade where BSL was so close above me, because I'm going to be hesitant to exit the put possibly prematurely. If there is a decent amount of distance between me and the BSL then I can close the trade for a loss a lot easier, as opposed to wait to see what happens at liquidity pool. @everyone

@everyone , RE: AMZN 125P,
 there wasn't much to this trade but I wanted to recap because it is an important part of the process and hopefully it will help you focus in on quality
I had AMZN on my watchlist due to its relatively flat pre-market price-action. After the strong rejection at open of the pre-market highs and the proximity of a strong 30min supply zone along with the macro data that came out, I knew we would be good for a downtrend. News is a good reason for TA to play out.

My entry point was the pLOD at 128.78 even though AH/PM lows were 129.51. I typically wait for a full close but I took the chance and front ran this one given that it was consolidating with increasing sell volume at pLOD and the broader market was selling off a bit.
I always try to maintain a 3:1 or 4:1 risk to reward ratio so I set my stop at the pre-market lows of 129.51 with a target of 126 which is a 1:3.75 risk to reward.

The tough question to answer is why I took profit early. The honest answer is that we were in the middle of a gap and I didn't want the price action to try to form a support level here. 
The closest possible support level would have been the pump and dump at split time but otherwise it was wide open. It ended up reversing in nowhere land at 126.87 with the broader market.
In hindsight, nothing on the 3 or 5min chart told me to exit, it was my gut.
In any situation, you want to buy into consolidation candles and sell into the large candles which is why I took profit at ~128.00 and a few more times a little lower. I should have waited longer but now I know for next time. 

Overall I netted 25% on this trade. 
I wish I had more hands, there were so many beautiful breaks today all around the same time, all you had to do was be ready with the buy button.
We can't catch them all, just glad we caught this one! Looking forward to tomorrow, its ZERO DAY! 🤘 (edited)

The primary concept annotated is **SMT Divergence (Smart Money Technique)** — marked twice on both charts, indicating deliberate non-confirmation between ES and NQ at key swing points. On the **lows (Monday session)**, ES made a lower low while NQ failed to confirm, signaling bullish SMT divergence. On the **highs (Tuesday session)**, ES made a higher high near **6,890** while NQ diverged, indicating bearish SMT divergence. The annotations **"W.O."** (likely **"Without")** appear on both charts near the highs, suggesting one instrument swept liquidity without the other confirming — a classic ICT manipulation signature.

FOMC Day!!! 

Remember a classic FOMC day is a 2 staged event, Leg 1 at the rate announcement (manipulation) and Leg 2 at the Powell Speech (True Move). 
You can trade either Leg or both but the highest probability trades come from trading Leg 2 after the HTF PDA has been tagged for the reversal to occur.

The W Profile of an FOMC Week should look like this, Consolidation into a Thursday Manipulation and Reversal, again the best trade is to wait for the Manipulation to form and trade the reversal from 1 side of the range to the next

These are the key levels im watching today for FOMC DOLs, NQ pHOW / REHs is my Ultimate DOL for the day on the 2nd Leg. I am assuming we follow HTFs and continue higher leading us to a manipulation lower and true move higher vs manipulation higher and true move lower. I will likely be looking for Longs from the ES D FVG or watching for any SMT inside the NQ D FVGs / pLOW. I would expect ES sweeps pLOW, NQ does not, and we reverse off that SMT / D FVG Tag for NQ pHOW REHs. If I am wrong and we manipulate higher first that is totally fine as I will only be trading Leg 2 today, I will wait for pHOW REHs sweep on NQ / -D FVG tag and look for the reversal lower to ES pLOW / D FVG

I will try and alert as I am trading but as we know FOMC is super fast, I will update this post before FOMC begins with the limits / TPs I am looking at. If you have any questions on today's plan as in # unknown-channel @everyone

### ICT Relevance:
From an **ICT methodology perspective**, these are critical **news kill zone events**. CPI and PPI releases are prime **manipulation windows** where smart money typically raids **BSL/SSL** before the true directional move. ICT traders would typically **avoid holding positions** into these releases or specifically hunt for **post-news displacement** and **FVG formation** immediately following the data prints as potential entry points.

FOMC Notes:

Some potential DOLs I'll be looking at for FOMC and what I'll be looking for in general.

-FOMC is normally a 2 staged event meaning the rate release offers one move (manipulation) and the Powell speech offers a reversal (true move). This doesnt play every time but it is what you should be looking for to fit your narrative on FOMC. 

-I am thinking we could have a Mid Week reversal this week but my W Bias is unclear as of now really. My main DOLs for FOMC are D VI / pLOD / D SSL RELs / NWOG. If we revisit NWOG today I see the case for the Mid Week Reversal / Wednesday Low of Week.

My bias heading into FOMC is likely a Judas higher on Rate Release into a drop to NWOG as Powell speaks, into EOW rally.

I will be quick to adjust bias if this is proved incorrect but this is what I will be looking for heading into the news drops. @everyone

### ICT Relevance:

Have to be very selective with the PM, and keep stops and TPs tight in order to account for the reduced volatility range. Trade Recap
Moving forward I will be looking for reversal based scalps to alert based off an old model I used to trade / alert. Here is what the model looks like / how you can be ready for the trades quickly with a custom OCO once I alert.

Asia SSL sweep on both indices, RTH Mitigation, 5m bullish candle printed after key Liq Sweep.
From here I drop to the 30s TF and hunt for entries
As soon as the 5m Candle closes Bullish I am looking for a 30s FVG to long
My SL is always 12 points aiming for 2R / 24 Points. If price is under the FVG I am looking to enter off of when the 5m candle closes I am still fine taking that entry. 
From here my TP2 if i chose to have one will likely be a 5m PDA / EQ of the current Dealing Range.
Optional: SL can Be set to BE after 8 points are achieved if risk adverse. In the past this is how I traded this model but moving forward I will be setting SL to BE at key MS
For study purposes, you can search on YT "ICT Cam's Model" for an explanation of this model. I have adjusted it and made it suit my personal needs to this rule set but the underlying model remains the same

How to join these trades with one click
I recommend if this model is something you'd like to join me in trading to have a custom OCO built on your platform with a 12 point SL aiming for 2R as this will always be my limits for these trades

Happy Trading!! 
@everyone (edited)

**[06/05/2025 12:45 PM]**
I wanted to recap this trade as while this is kgold, I customized it to the range for the most optimal entry, stop, and TP. This trade was A++. Huge sweep of London, RELs, and finally D SSL. Displacement up. I missed my very early 1m front run entry so waited for kgold to confirm on the 3/5m. While the IOFED on the 5m was higher, I had to make sure I was targeting IRL wisely, instead of a giant 2R without context to the range. 

Entry - I placed my limit in the 5m FVG CE. When price was retracing weakly I realized we were likely to reverse a few ticks before my entry. I immediately drug my limit line up and got filled as a slight front run to entry. Note: I see a lot of you joined me who were following the alerts, but some aren't tracking my updates in real time. I posted an alert the moment I took the slight front run and it offered time in the range to get in. Make sure to track # unknown-channel for entry changes and derisks, which will happen fast. 
Stop - I need to be targeting ~2R on kgold, so nestled my stop in the 3m OB, which price was unlikely to return to if we were going to expand. There was no need to protect all the way near lows, which would have forced a HUGE TP of likely 20 pts+

TP - Kgold is a IRL retracement strategy only, not a full reversal strat. I targeted right above the CE of the 15m FVG/1m bearish OB. That TP was our high for hours until the end of the day, saving 4 hours of waiting for potential $100/more a con. 

You can of course take kgold 100% mechanically and it will print, but adjusting your limits for the most optimal considerations for the range will make profitability fly

> **[Chart 5]** ## Economic Calendar Event Analysis

This image does **not show a trading chart** — it displays an **economic calendar entry**, specifically from a platform like Forex Factory or TradingView's economic calendar.

---

### Event Details:
- **Date/Time:** Friday, April 25, 10:00 AM (likely EST)
- **Currency:** USD
- **Event:** *Revised UoM (University of Michigan) Consumer Sentiment*
- **Forecast:** 50.8
- **Actual/Previous:** 50.6

---

### ICT Relevance:
In ICT methodology, this type of event is highly relevant as a **News Kill Zone catalyst**. The **10:00 AM EST window** falls within the **New York Kill Zone**, and UoM Consumer Sentiment is considered a **high/medium-impact news event** that can:
- **Expand volatility** to sweep **BSL or SSL** (buy/sell side liquidity)
- Create **displacement moves** and **Fair Value Gaps (FVGs)**
- Trigger **BOS or ChoCH** on lower timeframes

The slightly **lower actual (50.6) vs forecast (50.8)** suggests a **bearish USD sentiment miss**, which ICT traders would monitor for potential **liquidity sweeps** post-release.

### ICT Relevance
In ICT methodology, this falls within the **New York Kill Zone** (9:30–11:00 AM EST), and a **10:00 AM news release** is a known **high-impact macro event** that can cause **displacement, liquidity sweeps, and FVG creation**. ICT traders typically **avoid entries immediately before** this release and watch for post-news **displacement candles** targeting resting **BSL or SSL** on the higher timeframe.
